weather_report:
Subiaco settles into a damp, low-lit hush; clouds have rolled in and the first light rain is edging closer to town. Streetlights turn the wet bitumen into small rivers of pewter, and the air feels soft and cool. There will be no sunshine to speak of tonight, and the UV index is all but asleep; visibility is trimmed by drizzle and murk, a quiet curtain that makes everything feel nearer and slower. Showers look set to pass through around midnight and into the small hours, never fierce, just persistent enough to gloss the leaves and drum a gentle tempo on awnings.

Dress for a mild soak rather than a storm: a light rain jacket or hooded coat, shoes with grip you don’t mind wetting, and an umbrella if you prefer to keep your spectacles dry. It’s a night for small, companionable tasks—wipe the bike chain, sort tomorrow’s bag, brew something warm, or take a short, reflective stroll under the shopfront eaves if you need fresh air. Roads will be slick, so unhurried steps suit the mood best.
